Influencer Partners (KOL & Creator Ecosystem)

In the era where short-video and livestream economies have become mainstream,
traffic is no longer scarce, but high-quality monetization capability is still scarce.

The 360 ecosystem is a new-generation business model built on this trend—
bringing traffic back to value, and connecting content directly to assets and consumption scenarios.

I. What We Are Doing

360 Ecosystem is not just a content platform,
but a monetization system centered on “eco assets”:

- Land and eco-camp resources

- Made in China + compliant delivery in Australia

- Content → Scenario → Consumption → Revenue closed loop

You bring traffic, we provide real scenarios and products for sustainable monetization.

II. Who We Are Looking For

- KOLs / Influencers / Short-video creators

- Travel / Outdoor / Lifestyle bloggers

- International student content creators

- Overseas resource-based partners

- Founder-type IP / Personal brands

III. What Problems We Solve

Many people have traffic but cannot monetize it,
and many people create content but have no long-term income structure.

Core value of the 360 ecosystem:

- Convert traffic into real income

- Connect content to real consumption

- Turn one exposure into long-term value

IV. What You Can Gain After Joining

Based on your content style and resource matching direction:

- Camp experiences / Accommodation promotion

- Outdoor activities / Shooting scenarios

- Dining and lifestyle consumption

- Investment and asset-related content

Enter a content ecosystem with products, scenarios, and conversion.

V. Monetization and Revenue Mechanism

- Based on real assets and consumption scenarios

- Transparent and trackable revenue

- Revenue sharing based on conversion and participation

At the same time, you can also receive:

- 360 Smart Card membership benefits

- Priority access to internal resources

- Upgrade path to Black Card membership (premium benefits)

VI. Why Choose the 360 Ecosystem

- Real assets (Land & Eco Sites)

- Product capability (Cabin / Camp)

- Technical support (AI / IoT)

- Cross-border supply chain (Made in China + delivered in Australia)

Your content can be directly converted into assets and income.

VII. Collaboration Vision

In the future, what is most valuable is not traffic,
but the capability to turn traffic into value.

360 Ecosystem:
Content + Ecosystem + Assets + Revenue = Sustainable Growth
land_owner_join:
360 Eco Living System — Land Owner Partnership Guide

This document is based on the '360 Eco Living System (Operating Model Overview)' and provides a dedicated explanation of the Land Owner partnership model. It aims to help landowners understand how to transform their land into a sustainable cash-flow asset through the 360 eco-living system, while achieving long-term value growth.

I. Background & Pain Points

Australia has abundant land resources, but due to historical planning and government zoning management, different parcels of land are classified for different uses:

  • Residential Land
  • Temporary / Camping Land
  • Non-Residential Land (Rural / Conservation)

Many landowners face the following common challenges after purchasing land:

  • Unclear whether the land can be developed or used for habitation
  • Uncertain about how many people it can accommodate or how much income it can generate
  • Lack of compliance pathways and approval experience
  • No operational capability, leaving land idle or underutilised for long periods
II. The 360 Eco Living Solution

The 360 Eco Living System transforms land from a 'resource' into an 'asset' through integrated capabilities in assessment, design, operations, and traffic generation.

Core Logic:

Land → Compliance Assessment → Space Configuration → Environmental Development → Traffic Generation → Operational Revenue

III. Step One: Land Pre-Assessment

Before any development, the 360 team will conduct a systematic assessment of the land, including:

  • Land classification and zoning
  • Flood, bushfire and other risk factors
  • Surrounding environment and market potential
  • Number and types of spaces that can be accommodated
  • Power supply (grid connection or alternative energy sources)
  • Water supply (bore water, water storage, water delivery)

This stage determines whether the land is suitable for developing Eco Living assets.

IV. Step Two: Space & Asset Configuration

Based on land conditions and the owner's circumstances, suitable space products are configured:

  • Capsule (360 ZCamp)
  • Cabin
  • Glamping Tent
  • Zero-Carbon Energy Storage Space

An optimal combination plan is developed in line with the owner's financial capacity and investment expectations.

V. Step Three: Infrastructure & Environmental Development

A professional team completes infrastructure and eco-environment construction:

  • Water, power, road and other infrastructure
  • Gardens, planting and ecological landscaping
  • Outdoor activity and experience spaces

All works are completed by licensed electricians, plumbers, builders and other qualified tradespeople.

VI. Step Four: Technology Enablement (Key Advantage)

Modern technology is leveraged to solve traditional land development challenges:

  • Energy storage systems (solar / wind) to address off-grid power needs
  • Water resource solutions (bore water / storage / delivery) to address water supply needs
  • Drone and logistics networks to address supply issues in remote areas
  • AI systems for remote monitoring and operations management
  • IoT for intelligent device management
  • Self-service Check-in / Check-out systems enabling unmanned operations
VII. Step Five: Traffic Generation & Operations

Continuous traffic is generated through the 360 eco-system:

  • Founder IP and social media reach
  • Influencer / KOL collaborations
  • Music festivals, events and experience programmes
  • 360 Smart Card membership system

Unified platform operations ensure stable revenue generation.

VIII. Revenue & Value Growth

Landowners will benefit from:

  • Ongoing cash flow (accommodation + activities + consumer spending)
  • Significantly improved land utilisation
  • Increased land valuation (asset capitalisation)
  • Participation in revenue-sharing models

Additionally, through structured arrangements, further opportunities include:

  • Financing and loans
  • Investor introduction
  • Asset securitisation potential
IX. Summary

The core of the 360 Eco Living System is to upgrade traditional 'land' into an 'operable asset'.

Through professional assessment, space configuration, technology enablement and traffic operations, we help landowners move from:

'Holding Land' → 'Generating Revenue' → 'Achieving Asset Appreciation'.

how_it_works_page:
360 Eco Living System (Operating Model Overview)

This document explains the overall operating logic and business structure of the 360 Eco Living System. The system is built around five core elements: “Land + Space + Environment + Traffic + Operation”, and combines capital operations with technology capabilities to form a sustainable ecological asset and revenue model.

I. Phase One: Land (Land Owner)

Land is the foundation of the entire ecosystem. Land owners provide land resources, which is the first step in forming ecological assets.

1. Land Pre-assessment:

Assess land attributes (zoning, use restrictions, environmental conditions).
Assess surrounding conditions (transportation, landscape, market potential).
Assess infrastructure feasibility such as power supply, water supply, and drainage.

2. Compliance Feasibility Analysis:

Involvement of professionals such as Builders and Town Planners.
Determine whether it is suitable for developing Eco Living assets.
Develop a preliminary return on investment (ROI) model.

3. Government Approval (Council Approval):

Submit application and enter the compliance approval process.
Obtain legal construction and operation permits.

II. Phase Two: Space Supply (Space Provider)

Space providers are responsible for supplying modular living products, which are the core components of the assets.

1. Core Collaboration System:

360Space (space integration and design)
360ZCamp (core product supply system)

2. Product Types:

Capsule
Cabin
Glamping Tent
Energy Storage Space (zero-carbon energy storage type)

3. Core Work:

Match products according to land conditions
Compliance design and optimization
Manufacture and transport to Australia

III. Phase Three: Environment Setup

Build a complete ecological environment to improve experience and asset value.

1. Environmental Planning:

Natural landscape: flowers, fruit trees, planting
Functional facilities: roads, water systems, activity spaces
Experience content: camping areas, activity zones, etc.

2. Construction:

Completed by licensed electricians, plumbers, carpenters, builders, and other qualified personnel.

IV. Phase Four: Traffic & Ecosystem

Bring in users and consumption through multiple dimensions.

1. Content and IP:

Founder IP, influencer/KOL matrix

2. Activity System:

Music festivals, outdoor activities, Eco Living Program

3. Membership System:

360 Smart Card
Points circulation system
Consumption rights binding

V. Phase Five: Operation & Revenue

Unified operations to achieve stable revenue.

1. Operational Management:

Platform hosting, booking management, activity operations

2. Revenue Sources:

Accommodation, activities, catering, and retail

3. Profit-sharing Mechanism:

Revenue sharing among land owners, investors, and platform.

VI. Finance & Technology Enablement

1. Capital Operations:

Financial loans and financing support
Investor capital introduction and return mechanism
Asset securitization and long-term revenue model
Land integration (Land Bank)

2. Technology Enablement:

AI (Artificial Intelligence): user analysis, pricing optimization
IoT (Internet of Things): smart energy and equipment management
IT systems: integration of booking, membership, and operation platforms

3. Effects:

Improve operational efficiency
Reduce costs
Enable scalable replication

VII. Overall Ecological Closed Loop

Land → Space → Environment → Traffic → Revenue → Reinvestment

Form a continuously growing ecological asset system.

invest_in_eco_stays:
360 Eco Living System | Investment Overview

This overview is intended to help investors understand: how to acquire cash flow and asset appreciation opportunities in Australia by purchasing eco-living space assets. This model is based on "asset purchase + entrusted operations", not a financial investment product.

I. What Are We Doing? (Core Model)

We do not offer financial or wealth management products.

👉 What we provide:
Eco-living space assets deployed in Australia (e.g. Cabin / Capsule / Glamping Tent, etc.)

Investors purchase these assets and may choose to entrust us with operational management to generate potential returns.

II. Investment Structure (Compliant Framework)

This model consists of two clear steps:

① Asset Purchase
Investors purchase modular space products (payment may be made in RMB directly to Chinese suppliers or relevant companies)

② Entrusted Operations
Once assets are deployed in Australia, the 360 Eco System provides operational management services

👉 In essence:
Purchase of physical assets + entrusted operational services

III. Investment Thresholds

① Core Space Assets (long-term operations)
Approx. AUD $80,000 – $200,000

② Light Assets (tents / temporary spaces)
Approx. AUD $30,000 – $50,000
Shorter cycle (approx. 3–5 years)

IV. Returns Overview (Important)

Returns are derived from the actual operational performance of the asset, including:
• Accommodation revenue
• Activity and experience revenue
• Food & beverage and extended consumption

👉 Reference performance:
Based on historical and target operational data, some projects have achieved annualised performance of approximately 8%–10% (for reference only, not a guarantee)

👉 Upside potential:
Asset holders with existing customer networks or traffic capabilities may participate in additional revenue distribution

V. Why Does This Model Work?

• Suburban eco land is abundant but underutilised
• Modular spaces reduce development costs and timelines
• New energy and IoT technology address infrastructure challenges
• The 360 Eco System provides traffic and operational support

👉 Core:
Transforming land that previously generated no income into an operable asset

VI. Asset Value Growth Logic

Asset value is driven by:
• Sustained cash flow
• Occupancy rates and operational performance
• Eco support facilities (activities, dining, membership)

👉 Core logic:
Operations → Cash Flow → Asset Value

VII. Transparency & Management

• Operational data is trackable
• Revenue sources are clearly defined
• Management processes are standardised

👉 Emphasis: Real operations drive returns

VIII. Risk Disclosure (Compliance Statement)

• This project involves physical asset purchase and operational service arrangements
• It does not constitute a financial product or investment/wealth management advice
• Returns are the result of market-based operations and no fixed return is guaranteed
• Investors should make independent judgements based on their own circumstances

IX. Summary

We are not selling a financial product,
but providing a real, operable asset capable of generating cash flow.

asset_growth_roadmap:
360 Eco Living System — Asset Growth Roadmap Overview

This document explains how, within the 360 Eco Living System, land assets are gradually transformed from “idle resources” into “sustainable cash-flow assets”, and achieve long-term appreciation.

I. Core Concept
The core logic of the Asset Growth Roadmap is:
Not simply to develop a single project, but to enable land to continuously generate value and growth through systematic operations.

Logical Path:
Land → Compliance → Space → Environment → Traffic → Revenue → Appreciation

II. Real-World Problems (Status of Undeveloped Land)
Before development, most land faces the following problems:
• Unable to generate cash flow
• Difficult to maintain, with ongoing costs
• Long-term idleness causes value stagnation
• Natural environmental erosion (weeds, animals, climate impact)

Even if basic infrastructure is completed, without operations and traffic, it still cannot form sustainable revenue.

III. Stage One: Land Activation (From Resource to Developable Asset)
Through pre-assessment and compliance analysis, land gains a development foundation:
• Land attribute analysis (Zoning)
• Environmental and risk assessment (Flood / Bushfire)
• Water, power, and road feasibility
• Council approval pathway

Result:
Land shifts from “value cannot be determined” → “developable asset”

IV. Stage Two: Space Introduction (Forming Revenue Carriers)
Introduce modular living spaces (such as 360 ZCamp, Cabin, tents, etc.) to form operable units:
• Rapid deployment
• Compliant design
• Flexible combinations

Result:
Land shifts from “empty land” → “asset units with profit capability”

V. Stage Three: Environment and Experience Upgrade (Value Enhancement)
Enhance user experience through environmental development:
• Landscape and ecological design (flowers, planting, natural environment)
• Construction of activity and functional zones
• Infrastructure improvement

Result:
From “livable” → “willing to consume and stay”

VI. Stage Four: Traffic Introduction (Activating Cash Flow)
Introduce users through the ecosystem:
• Social media and IP traffic
• Influencers and events
• 360 Smart Card membership system

Result:
From “having products” → “having stable occupancy and consumption”

VII. Stage Five: Operations and Revenue (Forming Cash Flow)
Unified platform operations achieve continuous income:
• Accommodation revenue
• Event revenue
• Catering and extended consumption

Result:
Form stable cash flow

VIII. Asset Growth (Core Value)
Under continuous operations, assets enter the growth phase:
• Land valuation increases
• Revenue capability strengthens
• Financing capability improves
• Replicable expansion (multi-location deployment)

Key Logic:
Cash flow first → then valuation → then financing capability

IX. Summary
The Asset Growth Roadmap of the 360 Eco Living System is not short-term development, but a long-term operating model.

What we do is not simple construction, but:
Enable land to continuously generate income and keep appreciating.

aboutUs.section1.paragraph1: 35 years ago, this quiet land was a typical Australian farm , making the beginning of an exciting journey by its first owners , Henry and Norma. The land of Roseneath Holiday Park traces back to the mid-1990s, when it was under their ownership. At that time, they built the first house on the property and established the original septic system, forming the foundation of what would later become the park.
aboutUs.section1.paragraph2: Following this, the second owners, Terry and Grace Lewis, took over the property. They moved their whole family onto the land and carried out further development, gradually transforming it into a caravan park. The campsite was expanded to approximately 50 sites, welcoming more travellers to settle in and enjoy the beauty of nature. Their son, Gavin Lewis, still lives on the property and continues to support and service the park.
aboutUs.section1.paragraph3: The third owners, Grant and Karin Heeley, brought their own vision and dream to the land. They further expanded and improved the campsite, growing it to around 100 sites with enhanced facilities. Their dream was closely connected to the surrounding natural environment, particularly Lake Wellington, one of the largest lakes near the property, where they envisioned enjoying a lifestyle connected with nature and activities such as speedy boating. In 2020, the fourth owners, Danni and Kin Zhuo, arrived with their family for a short stay. Due to the pandemic lockdown, they found themselves unexpectedly “stuck” at the campsite. Over time, they fell in love with the land, chose to stay, welcomed two children, and brought with them a deep passion for outdoor travel and four-wheel drive (4WD) adventures across Australia and internationally, including Africa. This led to the creation of the brand “Little White Touring Australia”, bringing new energy and spirit into the park.
aboutUs.section1.paragraph4: Today, the fifth ownership phase is led by Dream Overseas Group, together with the World Cooperation Organization (WCO), represented by Mr. John Du and President Jerry Zhu, along with the Green Ess Wealth Family Fund team and Susan Gao Family Trust as part of the initial investor team. This new chapter brings a broader vision — integrating AI technology, smart systems, IoT (Internet of Things), and connectivity (WiFi) to transform the park into an eco-living destination in Australia. With a strong focus on low-carbon development, sustainability, and carbon-neutral initiatives, the park is being positioned not only as a holiday destination, but also as a demonstration site for innovation and environmental living. Under this vision, Roseneath Holiday Park is evolving into a flagship project within the group, aligned with the concept of the “360 Innovation & Incubation Park”, combining tourism, technology, sustainability, and global collaboration.
aboutUs.section1.location.title: Location & Environment
aboutUs.section1.location.content: Located on Woodpile Road (a no-through road), offering privacy and a secluded holiday experience. The park has access to Lake Wellington and is set in sandy land, bushland, banksia trees, native gums, flowering gums, birdlife, and sunset views. The park has two different moods: at times it can be busy and lively, and at other times it can be very quiet and peaceful.
aboutUs.section1.amenities.title: Amenities
aboutUs.section1.amenities.content: Children's playground, tennis court, heated swimming pool, and approximately 70 hectares of land. Suitable for boating, cycling, outdoor experiences, and other recreational activities.
aboutUs.section1.nature.title: Nature
aboutUs.section1.nature.content: The natural sandy environment makes it one of the rarer places that can support pet-friendly stays and fire pits, subject to conditions and park rules.
aboutUs.section1.shop.title: General Shop
aboutUs.section1.shop.content: Basic supplies including ice, bait, lollies, and essential items may be available.
aboutUs.section1.nearbyAttractions.title: Nearby Attractions
aboutUs.section1.nearbyAttractions.conte: Lakes Entrance, Metung Hot Springs, Sale Botanic Gardens, and other Gippsland destinations are within reach. Guests can also refer to the official website for further local tourism information.
aboutUs.section1.localStory.title: Local Story
aboutUs.section1.localStory.content: Guests may have the chance to meet members of the Roseneath family, including member of Lewis family, Gavin, and hear stories about the history of the park.
aboutUs.activities.paragraph1: The Roseneath Holiday Park is located in the beautiful, scenic Gippsland area, on the sandy, South East shore of Lake Wellington. This family and pet friendly Holiday park offers a real bush experience, being located in the tranquil bushlands of Meerileu.
aboutUs.activities.paragraph2: Experience fabulous fishing, boating and swimming. Fishing is a lot of fun and good catches of Bream, Mullet, Flathead, and Carp can be had. Fishing on the lake is great from the shore or a boat. Hollands Landing boat ramp is only a short drive from the park.
aboutUs.activities.paragraph3: You can relax with 3km of sandy, child friendly swimming beach on beautiful Lake Wellington. Enjoy the lake's clean, safe swimming conditions, which are ideal for yachting, canoeing, kayaking, jet skiing, and boating. Or take a long, lazy stroll along one of our many walking tracks or along the water's edge. You can also bring your pushbikes and enjoy one of the many bike tracks. The park is pet friendly and ponies and horses have a large paddock with clean water if you wish to bring them along and saddle up for some excellent trail riding.
aboutUs.activities.paragraph4: Get back to nature with some native flora and fauna spotting. Abundant wildlife includes rare Sea Eagles, Wedge Tail Eagles, friendly Parrots, and Rosellas. Wombats, Kangaroos, Wallabies, Rabbits, and Echidnas are easily found and large bush possums are everywhere and are easily hand-fed and friendly. Groups of Hog Deer are seen occasionally as they are fairly timid.
aboutUs.activities.paragraph5: We are located between Bairnsdale and Sale. The Park is set on 174 acres of peaceful bushland, including Banksia, Red Gum, White Gum, and ancient River Gum forest. The Park offers both powered and unpowered sites, a communal lounge, separate accommodation, sheltered BBQs as well as a camp kitchen. The park also offers a swimming pool and tennis court.
aboutUs.activities.paragraph6: Let the Holiday and camping experience rejuvenate and relax you and your family.
aboutUs.section2.title: Why People Visit
aboutUs.section2.intro: Escape busy city life and intensive work. Reconnect with nature. Enjoy fire pits, BBQ, water, beach, and lake activities. Easy access to other Gippsland attractions and resources. Strong internet coverage so guests can remain connected to work or friends. Group booking friendly. Flexible booking arrangements for different group sizes and budgets. Large open land suitable for events, animals, gatherings, and special activities.
aboutUs.section2.vision: Roseneath aims to be a leading AI-powered flexible stay venue for small bookings, busy periods, and large gatherings or special events.
aboutUs.section7.title: Flexible Accommodation Combination
aboutUs.section7.question: Can different accommodation types be combined?
aboutUs.section7.answer: Yes. One of the key advantages of Roseneath Holiday Park is flexibility. Guests can combine different accommodation types, such as: Big House + Bunk Room, Cabin + Bunk Room, Multiple Cabins for group bookings, Tiny Cabin + Bunk Room + Camp Site, Cabin + Camp Site.
aboutUs.section7.capacity: The park has capacity of about 50 under roof and camping capacity of over 1000 at any time also will increase from time to time. This allows groups of different sizes to customise their stay and optimise cost.
aboutUs.section7.vision: Roseneath aims to be a leading AI accommodation optimisation resort venue, bringing an 'IKEA-style' modular model into tourism to increase guest value for spend.
aboutUs.section8.title: Membership, Discounts, 360 Smart Card & Value Exchange
aboutUs.section8.membershipCard: RHP membership card powered by 360 Smart Card
aboutUs.section8.pricingQuestion: Can pricing vary or be discounted?
aboutUs.section8.pricingAnswer: Yes. Prices may vary depending on membership status, group bookings, special promotions, and seasonal demand. Guests are always welcome to enquire for the best available rate.
aboutUs.section8.ecosystem: Roseneath Holiday Park supports a membership ecosystem powered by 360 Smart Card. Key Functions: Membership identity, Discount eligibility, Booking privileges.
aboutUs.section8.barterSystem: Barter & Value Exchange System: The 360 Smart Card supports a local trading and value exchange ecosystem. Goods and services may be exchanged for accommodation value where applicable. Examples: Food suppliers may provide products and receive accommodation credit. Event partners may contribute services and receive stay value. Members and partners may participate in resource and value exchange where suitable.
aboutUs.section8.purpose: Support the local economy. Increase value circulation. Enable flexible tourism experiences.
aboutUs.section8.howItWorks: How does the 360 Smart Card work? Simply present your 360 Smart Card at Roseneath Holiday Park. Our office or shop will scan your card and deduct the value (points) directly for your purchase or stay.
aboutUs.section13.title: Activities, Events & Functions
aboutUs.section13.activities: Fishing, Outdoor recreation, Events, weddings, retreats (subject to approval)
aboutUs.section13.facilitiesQuestion: What activities or facilities are available?
aboutUs.section13.facilitiesAnswer: Depending on season and availability, the park may offer BBQ areas, public kitchen, toilets and showers, shop basics, fishing-related access, playground, pool, tennis court, event areas, and outdoor experiences.
aboutUs.section13.eventsQuestion: Can Roseneath host weddings, festivals, retreats, or group events?
aboutUs.section13.eventsAnswer: Yes. Roseneath also considers partnerships and group uses such as weddings, eco experiences, art projects, retreats, filming, festivals, and community events subject to approval.
aboutUs.section13.customEvents: Custom Events & Functions: Roseneath Holiday Park supports flexible event planning. We can work with guests to organise: Private functions, Weddings, Retreats, Group gatherings.
aboutUs.section13.catering: Options may include: International cuisine catering, Local food supply (meat, produce), BYO drinks, Group dining arrangements, Outdoor event setup.
aboutUs.section13.horsesAllowed: Horses are welcome. We are happy to work together with guests to design event plans based on group size, budget, and experience preference.
aboutUs.learnMore: If you want to learn more about Australian holiday parks, there are several options to consider. You can check out G'day Parks, which is Australia's largest network of holiday parks with over 300 parks across the country, offering discounts through their membership program. Big4 Holiday Parks is another great option with family-friendly facilities and accommodation options ranging from cabins to Holiday sites. Discovery Parks also offers various holiday experiences with special deals and discounts. You can explore these options further to find the best fit for your holiday needs.
about_story_page:
Our Story

The story goes back to 2008.

In a body of water in northern Sydney that could only be reached by ferry, an experience at a waterfront restaurant made founder John Du truly feel the charm of Australia’s natural way of living for the first time—away from the city, yet with a more real and quieter lifestyle.

It was also here that he purchased his first waterfront eco property.

This piece of land sits between mountain slopes and water, with steep terrain and a complex environment. At first, it was a purchase not fully understood. But over the following two decades, this land gradually became the starting point of long-term learning and accumulation.

Here, he learned how to live by the water, how to face changes in the natural environment, and how to understand the lifestyle of Australian communities. It was through this process that his understanding of eco living deepened—eco is not a concept, but a real way of life.

As time went on, this understanding continued to deepen.

In 2021, during the pandemic, John came across and acquired a representative eco asset—Chateau Le Marais, a private island French retreat castle. This is a fully off-grid eco space, with orchards, water sources, energy systems, and a complete natural cycle environment.

Here, life can operate by relying on nature itself. This experience moved the idea of eco living from “individual feeling” to “system understanding.”

In 2024, after long-term observation and preparation, the team further participated in the acquisition of Roseneath Peninsula Camp in Victoria. This is a caravan park with more than thirty years of operating history, with mature facilities and a stable customer base, as well as room for further development.

At this stage, eco was no longer only a personal experience, but began to show the possibility of replication and operation.

In this process, the joining of James D ing brought the team more professional operational capability. James graduated from a UK university and has 18 years of serviced apartment management experience in Australia, having managed high-end property operations in core areas such as Sydney and Brisbane. At the same time, he is also an enthusiast of eco living and owns his own farm on the Central Coast of NSW.

The combination of vision and experience gradually shaped a more complete direction for the team.

Through more than twenty years of accumulation, from a waterfront plot of land, to an island, and then to camps and farms, these experiences were not the result of a single decision, but of long-term practice.

The 360 ecosystem gradually took shape under this background.

It is not a concept that appeared suddenly, but a system that naturally extended from real land, real life experience, and long-term observation.

We believe:

Nature is not only a resource, but also long-term value;
Land can not only be owned, but also better utilized;
Eco living is not only an experience, but can also become a sustainable way of life.

The goal of 360 Eco is to systematize these resources, understandings, and capabilities based on real experience, so that more people have the opportunity to participate.

This is not an idea that started from zero, but a road that has already been traveled for a long time and continues to extend forward.

Roseneath Holiday Park – Official Policy & Rules

(Updated March 2026 )


Purpose and Application
This Policy applies to all persons within Roseneath Holiday Park, including permanent residents, annual users, short-stay guests, and visitors.

Nature of the Park
Roseneath Holiday Park is a privately operated, mixed-use, off-grid holiday park in a natural environment.

Ownership & Management Rights
Management retains full operational control, including rule updates, restricted areas, development, and commercial use.

Off-Grid Infrastructure
The park operates on solar, generator, bore water and limited systems. Interruptions may occur without liability.

Water & Utility Risk
Water may require treatment. Residents must use utilities responsibly and avoid overloading systems.

Registration and Entry
All persons must be registered prior to entering the park and must follow current access procedures.

Behaviour and Community Standards
All persons must respect others, follow management directions, and observe quiet hours from 10:00 PM to 7:00 AM.

Traffic & Safety
Speed limit 5 km/h. Only registered vehicles. No obstruction to emergency access.

Recreational Vehicles (Motorbikes, Quad Bikes, etc.)
The use of recreational vehicles, including motorbikes, quad bikes, and similar vehicles, is strictly regulated within the park.
Permitted usage time: 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM only
Outside these hours is strictly prohibited unless prior written approval is granted by management
All usage must be conducted in a safe and responsible manner, respecting other guests, residents, and the natural environment.
Failure to comply may result in:
Immediate restriction of use
Breach notice
Further action under park rules

Restricted Areas
For safety, environmental protection, operational control, and compliance purposes, certain areas within Roseneath Holiday Park are designated as Restricted Areas.
Residents and their guests must not enter, access, use, or pass through these areas unless prior written approval has been obtained from Park Management.
Restricted Areas include, but are not limited to:
Operational & Service Zones (Map 1) As shown on the park layout map (Map 1):
The area surrounding Tank 4 and Tank 5
The workshop, engine shed, diesel tank, and service yard
The private road and management/house compound
Any fenced, signed, or designated service infrastructure areas These areas are strictly for staff, maintenance, and authorised personnel only

Northern Environmental & Protection Zones (Map 2) As shown on the environmental/work map (Map 2):
All areas marked in yellow (Fire Protection Zone)
All areas marked in purple (Protected Land)
All areas marked in green (Modified Land – controlled use zones)
The northern sections identified for vegetation and environmental management (including rabbit and weed control zones)

These areas are subject to environmental restrictions, safety risks, and land management controls
General Restricted Areas Additionally, any area that is:
Marked as restricted on any official park map
Identified by signage, fencing, barriers, or markings
Designated by management from time to time is considered a Restricted Area.
Enforcement
Unauthorised access to Restricted Areas may result in:
Immediate direction to leave the area
Formal breach notice
Restriction of park privileges
Termination of residency (where applicable under law)

Off-Grid Environment
The park operates on solar, generator, and limited water systems. Responsible use of utilities and shared infrastructure is required.

Safety, Nature, and Environment
This is a natural bush, sandy, and lakeside environment. Wildlife may include snakes, insects, and other animals, including potentially dangerous or venomous species. All persons must take appropriate care for their own safety, remain alert, supervise children, and use suitable footwear, insect protection, and personal precautions.

All persons are encouraged to obtain their own suitable insurance to cover personal injury, accidents, belongings, and other risks. Except to the extent required by law, the park is not responsible for personal injury, wildlife-related incidents, or loss or damage to personal property.

Lake, Water Activity, and National Park Access Warning
The park is located near Lake Wellington and areas of natural environmental significance. Conditions on or near the lake may change quickly, including wind, tides, currents, and weather.

Any swimming, fishing, kayaking, boating, paddle activity, or other water sport is undertaken entirely at the user’s own risk. Users must remain within any warning or safety-marked areas where provided and are responsible for their own judgement, supervision, safety equipment, and conduct.
The park does not provide watercraft, boats, kayaks, swimming supervision, lifeguard services, or water-sport equipment unless expressly stated otherwise. Except to the extent required by law, the park accepts no responsibility for water-related accidents, injuries, loss, or damage.

Facilities and Public Amenities
All public amenities including toilets, camp kitchen, swimming pool, tennis court, and shared facilities must be kept clean and tidy after use.

If any damage, leakage, blockage, fault, or maintenance issue is identified, it must be reported to management immediately by mobile message or email.

All users must respect the natural environment and help keep the land clean, safe, and well maintained.

Cleanliness and Waste Management
Waste must be separated into:
General waste
Recycling (bottles and cans)
Cardboard

All users must separate waste properly to protect the environment. Cardboard may be reused for fire-starting where safe and appropriate.

Camping and Ground Protection
Camp users must not hammer nails, pegs, stakes, or similar objects into any restricted, marked, or protected ground areas as indicated by management.

If damage is caused to underground services, electrical systems, water pipes, irrigation, land, or park infrastructure, the responsible person will be liable for all repair and associated costs.

Fire Safety
Strict compliance with CFA rules. No open fires during bans. Safe storage of fuel required.

Pets
Pets must be controlled at all times and must not disturb other persons, wildlife, or the environment.

Security and Technology
CCTV, boom gates, digital systems, QR systems, and communication tools may be used within the park. All users must comply with these systems where applicable.

Site Works, Structures, and Alterations
All structural modifications, including decks, fences, annexes, awnings, sheds, washing lines, and similar external works, require prior management approval.
All approved works must comply with applicable council requirements, permits, and Australian building standards.
Any unapproved structure or alteration may be required to be removed, rectified, or reinstated at the occupier's cost.
Any damage, safety incident, or loss caused by renovation, construction, or unauthorised work is the responsibility of the person carrying out or authorising that work.

Site Appearance, Parking, and Property Care
Sites must be kept clean, tidy, and free from unreasonable clutter or hazards.
No abandoned, unregistered, unsafe, or non-operational vehicles, trailers, or equipment may be kept on site unless expressly approved by management.
Caravans and vehicles must be parked only in designated areas and must not obstruct roads, emergency access, neighbouring sites, or common areas.
Grass, garden areas, and external presentation must be reasonably maintained. Management may direct rectification where a site becomes overgrown, unsafe, or unsightly.
Park assets, furniture, signage, equipment, timber, trees, and natural resources must not be removed, damaged, cut, or collected without approval.

Utility Safety, Trades, and Tenant-Caused Damage
Tenants and occupiers should switch off power, water, and gas when leaving the park for extended periods where safe and practical to do so if apply.
Electrical, gas, plumbing, or similar works must only be carried out by appropriately licensed contractors and, where required, with prior management approval.

Visitors, Occupancy, and Length of Stay
Tenants and occupiers are responsible for the conduct of their guests and visitors at all times.
Visitors staying overnight must register with management and pay any applicable charges.
Management may refuse entry, restrict access, or direct a visitor to leave where there is a safety, compliance, behavioural, or operational concern.
Occupancy limits, approved use, and stay durations must be observed. Holiday or temporary stays must not be allowed to become permanent residency arrangements without management approval and proper legal documentation.
Management may monitor and record stay periods to ensure compliance with the Residential Tenancies Act 1997 (Vic) and related caravan park laws.

Compliance and Enforcement
Breaches of this Policy may result in warnings, restrictions, removal from the park, recovery of costs, or further action under applicable agreements or law.

Management Rights
Management may update rules, implement operational changes, and enforce safety or compliance requirements from time to time.

Acknowledgment and Contact
By entering, staying at, or occupying the park, all persons acknowledge that they are required to comply with this Policy, any lawful directions of management, and any applicable agreement.
Failure to comply may result in warnings, restrictions, charges, formal notices, termination processes, or legal action to the extent permitted by law.
For enquiries, contact management at info@roseneathholidaypark.au | Phone: (03) 4101 5333 | Website: www.roseneathholidaypark.au

Membership System / Membership (Eco Experience & Booking)

I. Membership System Overview

The 360 eco experience system provides membership mechanism support for all Guests who stay and participate in experiences,
unifiedly driven and managed by the 360 Smart Card.

This system integrates:
• Consumption discounts
• Points accumulation
• Resource sharing
• Value exchange

II. Core Membership Benefits

1. Exclusive discounts and booking benefits

When members book eco stays and experiences, they can enjoy:
• Member-exclusive prices and discounts
• Group booking discounts
• Priority participation in seasonal promotions
• Flexible booking arrangements (under certain conditions)

2. Points accumulation and spending mechanism

All consumption (accommodation, activities, services, etc.) can accumulate points:
• The more you spend, the more points you earn
• Points can be used for subsequent spending deductions
• Supports multi-scenario usage (accommodation / activities / partner merchants)

Unifiedly recorded and managed by the 360 Smart Card.

3. Smart card usage method

When members consume or book:
• Present or bind the 360 Smart Card
• The system automatically identifies membership status
• Automatically applies discounts or deducts points

Achieves a convenient integrated experience.

III. Value exchange and barter system

The 360 ecosystem supports a value exchange (Barter) mechanism:

Members or partners can use:
• Goods
• Services
• Resources

To exchange accommodation or experience value.

Typical scenarios:
• Food supply → exchange for accommodation
• Activity services → exchange for campsite use
• Electrician/plumbing/construction services → exchange for points or accommodation

This mechanism is used to:
• Promote resource circulation
• Support the local economy
• Improve overall utilization efficiency

IV. Applicable groups

This system applies to:
• Staying Guests (tourists)
• Service providers
• Partners
• Event organizers

Realizes the integration of consumption and participation.

V. Core philosophy

Members are not only consumers,
but also ecosystem participants and value creators.

The 360 membership system aims to build a continuously circulating ecological value system.

Roseneath Holiday Park – FAQ for Guest Bookings

SECTION 1 – ABOUT ROSENEATH HOLIDAY PARK

History: 35 years ago,  this quiet land was a typical Australian farm , making the beginning of  an exciting journey by its first owners , Henry and Norma.The land of Roseneath Holiday Park traces back to the mid-1990s, when it was under their ownership. At that time, they built the first house on the property and established the original septic system, forming the foundation of what would later become the park.Following this, the second owners, Terry and Grace Lewis, took over the property. They moved their whole family onto the land and carried out further development, gradually transforming it into a caravan park. The campsite was expanded to approximately 50 sites, welcoming more travellers to settle in and enjoy the beauty of nature.Their son, Gavin Lewis, still lives on the property and continues to support and service the park. The third owners, Grant and Karin Heeley, brought their own vision and dream to the land. They further expanded and improved the campsite, growing it to around 100 sites with enhanced facilities. Their dream was closely connected to the surrounding natural environment, particularly Lake Wellington, one of the largest lakes near the property, where they envisioned enjoying a lifestyle connected with nature and activities such as speedy boating. In 2020, the fourth owners, Danni and Kin Zhuo, arrived with their family for a short stay. Due to the pandemic lockdown, they found themselves unexpectedly “stuck” at the campsite. Over time, they fell in love with the land, chose to stay, welcomed two children, and brought with them a deep passion for outdoor travel and four-wheel drive (4WD) adventures across Australia and internationally, including Africa. This led to the creation of the brand “Little White Touring Australia”, bringing new energy and spirit into the park. Today, the fifth ownership phase is led by Dream Overseas Group, together with the World Cooperation Organization (WCO), represented by Mr. John Du and President Jerry Zhu, along with the Green Ess Wealth Family Fund team and Susan Gao Family Trust as part of the initial investor team.This new chapter brings a broader vision — integrating AI technology, smart systems, IoT (Internet of Things), and connectivity (WiFi) to transform the park into an eco-living destination in Australia. With a strong focus on low-carbon development, sustainability, and carbon-neutral initiatives, the park is being positioned not only as a holiday destination, but also as a demonstration site for innovation and environmental living.Under this vision, Roseneath Holiday Park is evolving into a flagship project within the group, aligned with the concept of the “360 Innovation & Incubation Park”, combining tourism, technology, sustainability, and global collaboration.

Location & Environment: Located on Woodpile Road (a no-through road), offering privacy and a secluded holiday experience. The park has access to Lake Wellington and is set in sandy land, bushland, banksia trees, birdlife, and sunset views. The park has two different moods: at times it can be busy and lively, and at other times it can be very quiet and peaceful.

Amenities: Children’s playground, tennis court, heated swimming pool, and approximately 70 hectares of land. Suitable for boating, cycling, outdoor experiences, and other recreational activities.

Nature: The natural sandy environment makes it one of the rarer places that can support pet-friendly stays and fire pits, subject to conditions and park rules.

General Shop: Basic supplies including ice, bait, lollies, and essential items may be available.

Nearby Attractions: Lakes Entrance, Metung Hot Springs, Sale Botanic Gardens, and other Gippsland destinations are within reach. Guests can also refer to the official website for further local tourism information.

Local Story: Guests may have the chance to meet members of the Roseneath family, including member of Lewis family, Gavin, and hear stories about the history of the park.

SECTION 2 – WHY PEOPLE VISIT

  • Escape busy city life and intensive work
  • Reconnect with nature
  • Enjoy fire pits, BBQ, water, beach, and lake activities
  • Easy access to other Gippsland attractions and resources
  • Strong internet coverage so guests can remain connected to work or friends
  • Group booking friendly
  • Flexible booking arrangements for different group sizes and budgets
  • Large open land suitable for events, animals, gatherings, and special activities
  • Roseneath aims to be a leading AI-powered flexible stay venue for small bookings, busy periods, and large gatherings or special events

SECTION 3 – INQUIRY → BOOKING → PAY → CHECK-IN → STAY → CHECK-OUT

Inquiry: QR code and AI systems may be used to provide high-efficiency answers to frequently asked questions. Q: How does AI or QR information work? A: The park may use AI Chat Support, QR information points, digital lockers, and online systems so guests can get information, ask questions, and complete parts of the booking or check-in process. Q: What happens after I book? A: You may receive a confirmation, payment request, check-in instructions, address details, and operational reminders. The park may also use temporary hold periods and payment links for booking confirmation.

Booking: Guests set dates for booking. In some cases, if a guest cannot make the booking, credit may remain available for up to two years or be transferable, subject to conditions. It may not be refundable within certain periods. Payment may be made directly to the park or through third-party platforms such as Airbnb, Booking.com, or Trip.com

Check-in: Daytime arrival is preferred. For later arrival, AI chat support, QR guidance, and map/instruction support may be available. Some staff may also be based in the park and assist where possible.

Stay: Guests can use the park as a base to explore local attractions and resorts. Please refer to the website for surrounding destinations.

Check-out: Guests should follow the provided departure instructions.

Additional AI Chat Support Functions: Answer accommodation questions automatically; Recommend best accommodation combinations based on group size, budget, and privacy preference; Provide pricing guidance; Send digital maps and arrival instructions; Assist with self check-in, especially for late arrivals.

SECTION 4 – ACCOMMODATION STRUCTURE

Q: What is the typical room structure across different accommodation types? A: All accommodation at Roseneath Holiday Park is designed with different room structures to suit various group sizes. Larger houses include living areas, multiple bedrooms, and full cooking facilities; Cabins include bedrooms, living areas, bathrooms, and essential kitchen facilities; Smaller units and camps provide more flexible and basic arrangements. This allows guests to choose or combine options based on their needs.

Big House

Q: How many bedrooms does the Big House have? A: The Big House can be configured as a five bedrooms house. Q: What bed types are provided in the Big House? A: Three bedrooms each contain one double-size bed, while the other two bedrooms each have two bunk beds. Each bunk bed can accommodate two people. Q: Does the Big House have private bathrooms? A: Yes. One bedroom includes an ensuite toilet, and there is one additional shared bathroom with shower for all guests. Q: What facilities are included in the Big House? A: The house includes a living room with a sofa and TV, as well as a fully equipped kitchen with cutlery, plates, cooking utensils, microwave, fridge and full cooking facilities. Q: How many guests can stay in the Big House? A: The Big House can accommodate up to 16 guests.

Additional Flexible Bunk Room (New Option)

Q: Is there an additional room option that can be used with the Big House? A: Yes. There is a separate 2-bedroom bunk room which can be used independently or together with other accommodations. Q: What is included in this bunk room? A: 2 bedrooms, 4 bunk beds, each bunk bed accommodates 2 people (total up to 8 guests). Bed-only setup (no bathroom, no shower, no kitchen facilities). Q: Does it have a separate entrance? A: Yes. It has independent access and a small outdoor garden area. Q: Can it be combined with the Big House? A: Yes. When combined with the Big House, it allows larger group arrangements and can support a maximum of around 16 guests depending on configuration. Q: Can it be combined with other accommodation types? A: Yes. It may also be combined with cabins or other booking arrangements for flexible group stays. Q: Are bedding options flexible? A: Yes. Guests can either use park-provided linen and bedding (additional cost may apply), or bring their own sleeping bag for a lower-cost option. Book here: https://book-directonline.com/properties/roseneathholidaypark-1?locale=en&roomTypeId=276181

2-Bedroom Cabin

Q: How many bedrooms are in the 2-Bedroom Cabin? A: The 2-Bedroom Cabin has two bedrooms for each separated room. Q: What beds are included in the 2-Bedroom Cabin? A: Each bedroom contains one double-size bed. Q: What facilities are provided in the 2-Bedroom Cabin? A: The cabin includes a bathroom, a living area with a sofa and TV, electric cooktop (desktop stove), microwave, fridge, plates, and utensils. Q: What is the maximum occupancy for the 2-Bedroom Cabin? A: The cabin can accommodate up to 4 guests. Book here: https://book-directonline.com/properties/roseneathholidaypark-1?locale=en&roomTypeId=276179

3-Bedroom Cabin

Q: How many bedrooms are in the 3-Bedroom Cabin? A: The 3-Bedroom Cabin has three bedrooms. Q: What bed configuration does the 3-Bedroom Cabin offer? A: One bedroom has a double-size bed, one has a single bed, and the third has one bunk (bottom double bed, top single bed) plus one single bed. Q: What facilities are included in the 3-Bedroom Cabin? A: It includes a bathroom, a living area with a sofa and TV, and a fully equipped kitchen with cutlery, microwave, fridge, plates, and utensils. Q: How many guests can stay in the 3-Bedroom Cabin? A: The cabin can accommodate up to 7 guests. Book here: https://book-directonline.com/properties/roseneathholidaypark-1?locale=en&roomTypeId=277501

Single Tiny Cabin

Q: What is included in the Single Tiny Cabin? A: The Single Tiny Cabin is a compact one-room unit. Q: What facilities are available in the Single Tiny Cabin? A: It includes air conditioning, a sofa, a double-size bed, and a kitchen equipped with cutlery, plates, outdoor cooktop, microwave, fridge and utensils. A cosy tiny cabin with a private deck, outdoor seating, fire pit, firewood box, and a private natural outlook surrounded by native Banksia trees. Q: How many guests can stay in the Single Tiny Cabin? A: It can accommodate up to a couple guests, and the base rate includes 2 guests. Book here: https://book-directonline.com/properties/roseneathholidaypark-1?locale=en&roomTypeId=289368

Powered Camp Sites

Q: Do Powered Camp sites include tents or accommodation structures? A: No. Guests must bring their own tent, caravan, or campervan. Q: How are Powered Camp sites allocated? A: Guests will be assigned to a designated site upon arrival. Q: How many guests can stay on one Powered Camp booking? A: Each booking can accommodate up to 4 guests. Q: What is included in the Powered Camp base rate? A: The base rate includes 2 guests. Q: What are the extra guest charges for Powered Camp sites? A: Additional guests are charged at AUD $22 per person per night, with children at half price. Book here: https://book-directonline.com/properties/roseneathholidaypark-1?room_type=288705

Unpowered Camp Sites

Q: Do Unpowered Camp sites include tents or rooms? A: No. Guests must bring their own camping equipment. Q: Is there a guest limit for Unpowered Camp bookings? A: There is no maximum guest limit per booking as the park can contain around 1000 people at any time. Q: What is included in the Unpowered Camp base rate? A: The base rate includes 2 guests. Book here: https://book-directonline.com/properties/roseneathholidaypark-1?locale=en&roomTypeId=277633

Additional Flexible Stay Options: Some accommodation options allow guests to choose between full service (linen included) or self-service (bring your own linen / sleeping bag). Rooms, bathrooms, toilets, and shared facilities are professionally cleaned and maintained by management.

SECTION 5 – CAPACITY

Approx. 50 guests under roof when accommodation is combined. Up to over 1000 guests with camping. Bed types available across the park include Double beds, Single beds, and Bunk beds.

SECTION 6 – PRICE AND RATE

Q: How is pricing calculated? A: Pricing ranges depending on accommodation type and season. From approximately $15 per person per night for unpowered camping up to approximately $100 per person per night for peak under-roof accommodation. Seasonal Structure: Off-Peak (1/5 – 31/10), Peak (1/11 – 30/4), and High Peak (Christmas, Easter). Children: Half price in most cases. Final pricing is subject to the booking system.

SECTION 7 – FLEXIBLE ACCOMMODATION COMBINATION

Q: Can different accommodation types be combined? A: Yes. Guests can combine different accommodation types like Big House + Bunk Room, Cabin + Bunk Room, or Tiny Cabin + Camp Site. Roseneath aims to be a leading AI accommodation optimisation resort venue, bringing an ‘IKEA-style’ modular model into tourism.

SECTION 8 – MEMBERSHIP, DISCOUNTS, 360 SMART CARD & VALUE EXCHANGE

Roseneath Holiday Park supports a membership ecosystem powered by 360 Smart Card. Key Functions: Membership identity, discount eligibility, and booking privileges. Barter & Value Exchange System: Goods and services may be exchanged for accommodation value. Food suppliers or event partners may contribute products/services and receive stay value. Q: How does the 360 Smart Card work? A: Simply present your 360 Smart Card; our office or shop will scan your card and deduct the value directly.

SECTION 9 – SITE & STAY

Q: Can I choose a specific site or location? A: Generally, sites are not pre-allocated; guests select available spots upon arrival. Earlier arrival provides better choice. Parking: Free parking with limited dedicated spaces.

SECTION 10 – WHAT TO BRING

Drinking water, extension leads for caravan, mosquito repellent, camping equipment, personal supplies, bedding, and cooking gear.

SECTION 11 – FACILITIES

Hot Water Shower & Toilets, Cold Water Showers at camp ground, Camp Kitchen, BBQ, Pool, Tennis court, Freezer & fridge. Commercial kitchen (available soon). General shop may provide ice, drinks, basics, and firewood. Note: No alcohol is sold on-site unless otherwise arranged for approved events.

SECTION 12 – SAFETY

Q: Are pets allowed? A: Yes, in approved bookings or areas. A pet fee may apply. Q: Are horses allowed? A: Yes, and fees apply. We offer a large horse paddock (approx 7 hectares). Q: Can I light a fire? A: Only if current CFA rules and park rules allow it. No open fires on total fire ban days. Q: Is it safe for children? A: Families are welcome; children must be supervised near roads, wildlife, and water. Q: Can I ride a motorbike? A: Only if management permits it in designated areas.

SECTION 13 – ACTIVITIES, EVENTS & FUNCTIONS

Fishing, outdoor recreation, events, weddings, retreats. Roseneath supports flexible event planning with options for international cuisine catering, local food supply, and outdoor setups. We are happy to work together with guests to design event plans.

SECTION 14 – RULES

Refer to the separate Roseneath Holiday Park Policy & Rules document.

SECTION 15 – CONTACT DETAILS

Roseneath Holiday Park: 422 Woodpile Rd, Meerlieu VIC 3862 | +61 3 4101 5333 | info@roseneathholidaypark.au. Melbourne Office: Level 2, 171 La Trobe Street, Melbourne VIC 3000 | John Du | +61 (04)1316 8533 | corp@roseneathholidaypark.au.

FINAL NOTE – CORE PRINCIPLE OF THE PARK

Roseneath Holiday Park is built on flexibility, integration of technology (AI/QR), resource sharing, support for local economy, and creation of meaningful experiences. The combination of technology, flexible accommodation, and value exchange creates a tourism model designed for the future.

Events & Activities – A Multicultural Platform

Australia is one of the most culturally diverse nations in the world, with over 200 ethnic communities and more than 250 languages and dialects. This diversity is a core backbone of the country, supported by government policy, contributing to social cohesion, innovation, and economic growth.

From 23rd to 26th January 2026, Roseneath Holiday Park successfully collaborated with PSYCHOMANIA by Nezumi Productions and 360 Culture & Events Perpetual Limited to deliver a multicultural music and lifestyle gathering. The event attracted over 600 participants from across Australia and internationally, including artists from more than 10 countries.

Roseneath Holiday Park offers a unique open-ground environment combining nature, camping, accommodation, food, and cultural expression into a holistic outdoor experience.

Multicultural events bring significant value, including mental wellbeing, community connection, local economic growth, employment opportunities, and regional activation. These outcomes align with government priorities in multicultural development and tourism.

We are seeking opportunities to collaborate across Australia on public and private land. This model creates value for landowners through increased utilisation, additional revenue, and long-term partnerships.

All events are conducted under strict compliance, including council approvals, safety management, security, and full regulatory requirements.

This platform represents a scalable model for culture, community, sustainability, and innovation, positioning Roseneath Holiday Park as a leading eco-living and multicultural destination in Australia.

Innovation Lab – Roseneath Holiday Park

Roseneath Holiday Park, in collaboration with 360 Innovation & Incubation Park (360 IIP), is building one of Australia’s leading innovation demonstration bases and live testing grounds.
This collaboration positions the park as an experimental platform that integrates natural environments with technology applications to explore future eco-living and smart tourism models.

Core Focus Areas:
- AI and digital systems (automated booking, smart front desk, AI Q&A)
- Internet of Things (IoT) management systems
- Renewable energy (solar systems and energy optimisation)
- Water systems (filtration, irrigation, and sustainable management)
- Modular and relocatable accommodation products
- Environmental protection and low-carbon (carbon-neutral) development
- Smart tourism and automated operations

Infrastructure and Technology Development:
- Digital lockers, smart access control, and automated check-in systems
- Starlink network and communication systems
- Electricity metering, water, and gas systems
- Security and data management systems
- Upgrades to public facilities and wellness spaces

This innovation model integrates:
- Technology
- Tourism
- Sustainable development
- Community value

Industry Collaboration:
We welcome the following partners:
- Australian local enterprises
- International manufacturers
- Technology and equipment suppliers (lighting, security, modular housing, IoT systems, etc.)

In particular, modular eco-living products will be tested and applied in this project.

National Replication and Promotion:
This model can be replicated to:
- Caravan parks
- Farms
- Tourism real estate
- Public and private land
It provides a practical operating reference model for landowners and operators.

Economic and Social Value:
- Job creation
- Regional economic development
- Tourism value enhancement
- Sustainable innovation promotion

Education and Talent Development:
It provides a practical platform for university students and graduates to apply theory in engineering, IT, tourism, and environmental fields.

Additional Update:
This project has already started and will progressively benefit other farms; it spans IoT, AI, goods, products, services, solar, and trade services, with collaboration from national level to council level, and updates will be added from time to time.

Vision:
To develop Roseneath Holiday Park into one of Australia’s leading eco-living and innovation demonstration bases, achieving integrated growth across technology, environmental protection, and tourism.
